meta_title: For Freelancers: client workflow to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Solve Debugging Bottlenecks with AI Workflows
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with cryptic bugs. Learn a high-speed freelancer workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to debug faster, impress clients, and ship reliably.
content.answer_block: Stop manually tracing logs. Solve debugging bottlenecks by integrating Antigravity for automated diagnostic mapping and Claude Code for iterative, context-aware remediation. By treating debugging as a structured pipeline rather than a reactive task, you reduce mean-time-to-repair (MTTR) by 70%, ensuring your client projects remain profitable and high-quality.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is pinging you on Slack, and your terminal is bleeding red errors. You’ve refactored the same function four times, yet the bug persists, mocking your reputation as a senior developer. This isn't just a coding error; it's a productivity death spiral that kills your margins.

But what if you stopped fighting the code and started orchestrating it? By shifting from manual trial-and-error to an AI-assisted diagnostic workflow, you reclaim your authority and your time. This guide is your blueprint for transforming debugging from a high-stress bottleneck into a predictable, automated process.

steps.title: Establish Diagnostic Context
steps.description: Before fixing, you must map the execution flow. Use Antigravity to visualize the dependency graph and identify where state corruption is likely occurring.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current execution path for file [filename] and identify potential race conditions or state inconsistencies between the API layer and the database model.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --read src/controllers/ --context 'Identify logic gaps in state management'

steps.title: Automated Error Traceability
steps.description: Feed your stack trace into the AI to pinpoint the exact line of failure within your complex middleware stack.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Ingest the following stack trace and map it to the specific function call hierarchy in the codebase to isolate the root cause.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --task 'Analyze this stack trace and suggest a patch: [paste stack trace]'

steps.title: Isolate the Logic Branch
steps.description: Create a minimal reproduction script to ensure your fix doesn't introduce regressions in other parts of the client's application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a isolated unit test scenario that triggers the identified bug without requiring the full application runtime.

steps.title: Iterative Remediation
steps.description: Apply the fix using Claude Code's agentic capabilities, ensuring all side effects are considered before committing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose a refactor for [function] that fixes the bug while improving performance and adhering to the existing design pattern.

steps.title: Regression Verification
steps.description: Run the full test suite and use Antigravity to verify that no downstream dependencies were negatively impacted by the code changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the recent changes in the codebase and verify if any other modules rely on the modified function signature.

pro_tips: Always commit your state before triggering an AI agent to allow for atomic rollbacks.
Use Antigravity to generate documentation for your fixes; clients love seeing 'Technical Debt Reduction' in your status reports.
Keep your Claude Code context window lean by excluding large vendor folders in your .claudeignore file.
Treat AI suggestions as 'senior-level code reviews' rather than 'answers' to maintain your architectural oversight.
Use the 'explain' command in Claude Code to document why a specific fix was chosen for your client's future maintenance.
resources: Best Practices for AI Engineering: https://ai-builder-community.com/docs
content.next_steps: Now that you've mastered the diagnostic workflow, audit your current project for three recurring bugs and apply this five-step process to each one to validate your new speed.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs drain your billable hours and start delivering high-velocity, high-quality code that keeps clients coming back for more. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
